blocks.content: Nathan finishes the tie. He steps back and examines his work with the expression of a man who has achieved something.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: You look good. Very marriageable. Cassandra's going to fall for you all over again.
Nathan picks up his own jacket from the chair and shrugs into it, adjusting the collar in the mirror beside Ethan.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: We're not doing this right now.
Nathan pauses with his collar half-turned down.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: The penguin is a household object at this point.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: She gave it to you on the second date.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: She gave it to me at the carnival. That barely counts as a date.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: She came to your apartment specifically to give it to you.
Nathan finishes his collar. He straightens up. He looks at himself in the mirror with the expression of a man who has no rebuttal.
Nathan straightens his jacket one more time. He looks at Ethan in the mirror and something genuine moves through his face — the warmth that lives underneath all the theater of him.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: She's going to walk through that door and you're going to forget everything you were just thinking about.
